title_en: Key Themes: Revelation, Prophetic Mission, and the Formation of the Muslim Community
title_id: Tema-Tema Utama: Wahyu, Misi Kenabian, dan Pembentukan Komunitas Muslim
content_en:
Al-Fusul fi Sirat ar-Rasul covers the essential narrative arc of the prophetic mission with a focus on the theological and historical significance of each phase. Ibn Kathir is not simply reporting events but drawing out their meaning for Muslim understanding of prophethood, divine guidance, and the emergence of the Muslim community.

The sections on the beginning of revelation (nuzul al-wahy) are particularly carefully handled. Ibn Kathir presents the accounts of the first revelations in the Cave of Hira with attention to the chain of transmission and the variant reports preserved in the hadith literature. His discussion addresses the Prophet's ﷺ initial state, Khadijah's response, the role of Waraqah ibn Nawfal, and the period of the cessation of revelation (fatrah), drawing on the most reliable narrations to establish what can be known with confidence about these momentous events.

The Meccan period is treated with attention to the dynamics of prophetic preaching and opposition. Ibn Kathir's account of the early converts, the persecution of the Muslims, the various emigrations to Abyssinia, and the social and economic pressures applied to the nascent Muslim community reflects his reading of this period as a trial through which faith was tested and purified. The section on the Year of Sorrow — the deaths of Khadijah and Abu Talib — is presented with appropriate gravity as a turning point that preceded the divine authorization of the Hijrah.

The Medinan sections of Al-Fusul cover the battles and expeditions of the Medinan period with scholarly rigor. Ibn Kathir is careful to distinguish between well-attested accounts of the major battles — Badr, Uhud, al-Khandaq, Khaybar, Makkah — and embellished or unreliable details that have accumulated in popular tradition. His critical notes in these sections are a valuable guide for students navigating the vast literature on these events.

The concluding sections on the Prophet's ﷺ final illness and death (wafah) are among the most moving in the work. Ibn Kathir presents the accounts of the Prophet's ﷺ last days, his final sermon, his instructions to the community, and the response of the Companions to his passing with the reverence and care appropriate to narrating the most significant departure in Islamic history.
content_id:
Al-Fusul fi Sirat ar-Rasul mencakup alur narasi inti misi kenabian dengan fokus pada signifikansi teologis dan historis setiap fase. Ibn Kathir tidak sekadar melaporkan peristiwa, tetapi menggali maknanya bagi pemahaman Muslim tentang kenabian, bimbingan ilahi, dan kemunculan komunitas Muslim.

Bagian-bagian tentang permulaan wahyu (nuzul al-wahy) ditangani dengan sangat cermat. Ibn Kathir menyajikan kisah-kisah wahyu pertama di Gua Hira dengan memperhatikan sanad dan riwayat-riwayat yang berbeda yang tersimpan dalam literatur hadis. Pembahasannya meliputi kondisi awal Nabi ﷺ, respons Khadijah, peran Waraqah ibn Nawfal, dan masa terputusnya wahyu (fatrah), dengan bersandar pada riwayat-riwayat paling terpercaya untuk menetapkan apa yang dapat diketahui dengan keyakinan tentang peristiwa-peristiwa monumental ini.

Periode Makkah dibahas dengan memperhatikan dinamika dakwah kenabian dan penentangan terhadapnya. Kisah Ibn Kathir tentang para pemeluk Islam awal, penganiayaan terhadap kaum Muslim, berbagai hijrah ke Habasyah, serta tekanan sosial dan ekonomi yang diterapkan terhadap komunitas Muslim yang baru tumbuh mencerminkan pembacaannya terhadap periode ini sebagai ujian yang memurnikan iman. Bagian tentang Tahun Kesedihan — meninggalnya Khadijah dan Abu Thalib — disajikan dengan kesungguhan yang layak sebagai titik balik yang mendahului izin ilahi untuk hijrah.

Bagian-bagian Madinah dari Al-Fusul mencakup perang-perang dan ekspedisi pada periode Madinah dengan ketelitian ilmiah. Ibn Kathir berhati-hati membedakan antara kisah-kisah yang sahih tentang perang-perang besar — Badr, Uhud, al-Khandaq, Khaybar, Makkah — dan perincian yang dilebih-lebihkan atau tidak dapat dipercaya yang telah terakumulasi dalam tradisi populer. Catatan-catatan kritisnya dalam bagian-bagian ini adalah panduan berharga bagi para pelajar yang menelusuri literatur yang sangat luas tentang peristiwa-peristiwa tersebut.

Bagian-bagian penutup tentang sakit dan wafatnya Nabi ﷺ adalah yang paling mengharukan dalam karya ini. Ibn Kathir menyajikan kisah-kisah tentang hari-hari terakhir Nabi ﷺ, khutbah terakhir beliau, wasiat beliau kepada umat, dan respons para sahabat atas kepergian beliau dengan rasa hormat dan kehati-hatian yang pantas untuk meriwayatkan kepergian paling agung dalam sejarah Islam.
content_ar: Al-Fusul fi الصراط ar-Rasul covers the الجوهري narrative arc of the prophetic mission with a focus on the theological and historical significance of each phase. Ibn Kathir is not simply reporting events but drawing out their meaning for مسلم الفهم of prophethood, divine الهداية, and the emergence of the مسلم الجماعة. The sections on the beginning of الوحي (nuzul al-wahy) are particularly carefully handled. Ibn Kathir presents the accounts of the أولاً revelations in the Cave of Hira with attention to the chain of transmission and the variant reports preserved in the الحديث literature. His discussion addresses النبي ﷺ's ﷺ initial state, Khadijah's response, the role of Waraqah ibn Nawfal, and the period of the cessation of الوحي (fatrah), drawing on the most reliable narrations to establish what can be known with confidence about these momentous events. The Meccan period is treated with attention to the dynamics of prophetic preaching and opposition. Ibn Kathir's account of the early converts, the persecution of the المسلمون, the various emigrations to Abyssinia, and the social and economic pressures applied to the nascent مسلم الجماعة reflects his reading of this period as a الابتلاء through which الإيمان was tested and purified. The الفصل on the Year of Sorrow — the deaths of Khadijah and Abu Talib — is presented with appropriate gravity as a turning point that preceded the divine authorization of the Hijrah. The Medinan sections of Al-Fusul cover the battles and expeditions of the Medinan period with العلمي rigor. Ibn Kathir is careful to distinguish between well-attested accounts of the major battles — Badr, Uhud, al-Khandaq, Khaybar, مكة المكرمة — and embellished or unreliable details that have accumulated in popular التراث. His البالغ الأهمية notes in these sections are a valuable guide for students navigating the vast literature on these events. The concluding sections on النبي ﷺ's ﷺ final المرض and الموت (wafah) are among the most moving in the work. Ibn Kathir presents the accounts of النبي ﷺ's ﷺ last days, his final sermon, his instructions to the الجماعة, and the response of the الصحابة to his passing with the reverence and care appropriate to narrating the most المهم departure in Islamic history.
